description_long: This is a vintage, single-propeller monoplane with a light brown fuselage and wings, featuring prominent roundels in red, white, and blue on the wings and tail, indicative of early 20th-century military aircraft. The wings are straight and mounted at a mid-level position, supported by visible struts and wires. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, with a single visible pilot figure. The landing gear consists of two large, fixed wheels at the front and a small tail skid at the rear. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer with a tricolor rudder and a horizontal stabilizer, both in matching colors.
description: A vintage, single-propeller monoplane with brown wings and fuselage, open cockpit, and colorful roundels on the wings and tail.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the propeller, open cockpit, and roundels on the wings and tail.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tail section, tricolor rudder, and the full span of the wings with roundels.
description_view_2: Rear-right view focusing on the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and cockpit placement.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the propeller, landing gear, and open cockpit with pilot figure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing monoplane with straight, braced wings and no winglets; supported by external struts and wires.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer; tricolor rudder.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit directly behind the engine; single-seat configuration with no canopy.
